transcript: Yes. He had risen through the ranks of Egypt, so that he was the prime minister of Egypt next to the Pharaoh.
[laughs] Let me tell you guys a story.
Oh, I... I... I think that, uh, things should be censored. I don't think they should be, uh... uh, necessarily bring--
--that many. You know, I think the law should, uh, do something about people coming to town and just saying whatever they please. Especially to young people.
Let's get some, uh, thoughts from, uh, John, here in Lakewood. Hi John.

Yeah, uhhh, I don't... I just... I don't know. First of all, I think, um, "censorship," and realize this has pretty much been beaten to death. With 2 Live Crew, you know, they've got so many other things to worry about besides, you know, the heyday of, uh, of the negative press they've been getting. Uh, they've got helium to worry about now, which is something, you know, that's just, uh... opened up a whole new ball park for them.
Right. And, um, now they've got, you know, mallets on stage, you know, where... where are they... where are they gonna stop? That's what my question is.
[laughs] Yeah, I guess, uh, the next thing will be, uh, sacrificing, uh, human beings on stage.
But then, you see, they'd be breaking the law. Then that would be killing.
Yeah, but I mean at this point they're... they've already, ummm, they've already begun to use helium. I just don't see the valid... the validity in that. Do you... do you have any insight on that?
No, uh, using helium in what way?
Well, I mean how would they use helium?
You mean they suck it in and they talk?

And then they... they... um, they actually mount lambs. They saddle lambs on stage, and I don't... you know, I don't... I don't see this as entertainment of any form. Uhh, I... I may be wrong, but I think... I think--
--it is true the cooler you are, the more willing you are to accept it.
Yeah, well, I guess, uh, it's in the eyes of the beholder, I suppose--
--as to what is art and what is entertainment, so, uh, you know, if you don't like that kind of act, you don't have to go see it, I guess.
But... But the lamb thing is what really strikes me as being kind of odd. I mean, how--
Well, if... if they're hurting... if they're hurting the lamb, then, uh, then perhaps, uh, animal cruelty charges can be filed.
Well, see, that's the thing. They're giving the helium to the lambs, that's... that's where it's kinda like, uh, psyches out...
And... and... and... wha... and... and you're talking about 2 Live Crew?
Right. Uh, and in fact I think it's part of this leg of the tour that's gonna be through Denver. An--

Well, then perhaps they ought to be slapped with, uh, with a charge of, uh, of cruelty to animals if they can prove that helium is, uh, you know, if, uh, if... if giving helium to a lamb is... is, uh, harmful.
Yeah, and then they begin to use the mallets on the lamb...
And I don't understand that one bit.
Well, then that is cruelty, and I... I would... I would disagree with that, but uhh, uhh, I... I'm saying the performance of the music itself, uhh, there's no reason why we should censor it.
Sure, absolutely, but one thing, how do you feel about at the end of the show where they strike a gong with a lamb? That--
Now, I have never seen that.
That-- that is part of their live show, and I don't... I don't think that's a viable thing for any artist to do at this point.
Man! I... see, I didn't know that. You've... you've enlightened me here, John.
